item_text: the diagram models the flow of a device scan, where the main application executes the device scan, which then checks if the hardware is functioning. if it is, it retrieves the impact of component replacement; otherwise, it returns a result with the name, impact component, and diagnostic device. the flow is a sequence of events that can be tested by checking the conditions and branches. the diagram does not show any loops or repetitions. the note suggests that if there are defective components, the impact of the change should be calculated.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-hardwarescan-001 title: successful hardware scan module: hardwarescan source pages: 4,5 preconditions: user is logged in. test data: - device: laptop test steps: 1. user initiates a hardware scan. 2. application sends a message to the hardware to execute a scan. 3. hardware responds with component speed and health. 4. application calculates the expected life of the components. expected result: hardware scan completes successfully and expected life is calculated.

best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-registration-001 title: user registration with valid credentials module: registration source pages: 1,2 preconditions: user is not logged in. test data: - username: testuser - password: testpass123 - email: testuser@example.com - admin: false test steps: 1. user enters valid credentials (testuser, testpass123, testuser@example.com, false) into the registration form. 2. user clicks on the "register" button. 3. application verifies the user's credentials. 4. application adds the user to the database. expected result: user is successfully registered and redirected to the dashboard.
